data.title: Asia stocks edge up ahead of Fed policy outcome and heavyweight earnings
data.description:
Asian equities traded mostly higher on Wednesday as investors positioned ahead of the US Federal Reserve policy decision and major earnings releases.

Gold prices hovered below $4,600 an ounce after dropping nearly 2% in the previous session.

WTI crude futures held above $99 per barrel after gaining more than 3% in the prior session.

The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.5% to around 6,670, resuming its record rally after opening lower.

Japan (NKY:IND) market closed. The Japanese yen hovered around 159.6 per dollar in holiday trading on Wednesday.

China's (SHCOMP) rose 0.49% to 4,084, while the Shenzhen Component Index gained 0.3% to 14,880, and the offshore yuan traded around 6.83 per dollar, remaining near a recent more than three-year high.

Tensions also rose after Meta Platforms Inc. was prevented from acquiring AI startup Manus, highlighting Beijing’s focus on controlling key technologies.

China will step up state backing to expand capacity and upgrade quality in the service sector, as it deepens reforms, taps technology, and broadens opening-up to drive growth. 

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 1.37% to around 25,985, rebounding from the previous session’s losses.

India (SENSEX) rose 1.21% to 77,221, recovering from losses in the previous session.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.35% to 8,666, marking a seventh straight session of losses and the lowest in three weeks. The Australian dollar edged down to below $0.72 but stayed near four-year highs as a sharp rise in inflation kept expectations of a rate hike next week.

Australia’s annual inflation rose to 4.6% in March 2026 from 3.7% in February, the highest since September 2023 and above the central bank’s 2–3% target, though below expectations of 4.8%. 

In the U.S. on Tuesday, all three major indexes ended lower tech companies weighed on U.S. stock indices as sharp losses for AI companies offset robustness in other sectors ahead of major earnings reports.

U.S. stock futures moved modestly higher as investors turned their focus to a fresh batch of big tech earnings and the upcoming Federal Reserve policy announcement: Dow +0.08%; S&P 500 -0.01%; Nasdaq -0.18%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Syntec Optics prices $20M stock offering at $7 per share
data.description:
• Syntec Optics (OPTX) said it has priced an underwritten public offering of 2.86M shares of common stock at $7.00 per share.
  • The gross proceeds are expected to be $20M.
  • The offering includes a 30-day option for the underwriter to purchase up to 428,571 additional shares, potentially increasing total proceeds.
  • The net proceeds are expected to be used for working capital, potential debt repayment, and investments or acquisitions, supporting the company’s growth strategy.
  • The transaction is expected to close around April 30, 2026.

data.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face off in court in a high-stakes AI showdown
data.description:
On the second day of trial, Elon Musk reiterated a range of remedies he has pursued since filing his 2024 lawsuit, alleging they went back on their promises to keep the artificial intelligence lab a nonprofit.

In January 2026, Musk claimed seeking up to $134 billion in alleged “wrongful gains.” Most recently, he has called for the removal of Sam Altman and Greg Brockman from their roles at OpenAI.  

OpenAI established a for-profit subsidiary after Musk left its board in 2018, and Musk claims that his $38 million worth of contributions to the company were used for unauthorized commercial purposes.

In their opening statement, OpenAI’s lawyers said, Elon Musk never insisted OpenAI stay nonprofit. An email from Shivon Zilis to Sam Teller showed options to shift to a for-profit or hybrid model. Savitt argued Musk supported a for-profit only if he had control and didn’t fully deliver his pledged funding.

During his testimony on Tuesday, Elon Musk repeatedly stressed that he founded OpenAI to act as a counterbalance to Google. He said the idea emerged after a dispute over AI safety with Google co-founder Larry Page. Musk testified that he believed Page was not taking AI safety seriously, which led him to pursue a nonprofit, open-source alternative to Google.

Testimony has concluded for the day, with Musk set to return to the stand on Wednesday to continue.

data.title: Borealis Foods replaces Frontwell debt with $17M Oxus financing
data.description:
• Borealis Foods (BRLS) entered into a $17M refinancing agreement with Oxus Capital, using the proceeds to fully repay its existing Frontwell Capital credit facility and strengthen near-term liquidity.
  • The new secured term loan carries a 12% annual interest rate and matures on April 27, 2031.
  • The proceeds of the term toan were used to repay in full all outstanding obligations under the company’s existing credit agreement with Frontwell.
  • In connection to the refinancing, the company also entered a separate conversion agreement that could see roughly $29M of related-party debt convert into equity if the company fails to raise at least $70M in new equity at $9 per share by July 1, 2026.
  • The credit agreement also requires the company to appoint two designees of Oxus to the Board of Directors by May 11, 2026, replacing two existing directors.

data.title: Deutsche Bank beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank press release (DB): Q1 GAAP EPS of €1.06 beats by €0.14.
  • Revenue of €8.67B (+1.8% Y/Y) beats by €110M.
  • Post-tax profit rose 8% year on year to a quarterly record of €2.2 billion.
  • 12.7% post-tax return on tangible equity, up from 11.9% in Q1 2025.
  • 58.9% cost/income ratio, down from 61.2% in the prior year quarter.
  • Post-tax RoTE near or above 13% in all four businesses.
  • Assets under management rise to €1.8 trillion including net inflows of €22 billion.
  • Provision for credit losses of €519 million, 43 bps of average loans.
  • Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 13.8%, within target operating range of 13.5-14.0%.
  • Previously-announced €1 billion share buyback underway.
  • Outlook for 2026: Revenues: first-quarter performance in line with expectations, providing a solid step-off point for the bank’s full-year 2026 revenue ambition of €33 billion.
  • Costs: expenses expected to be in line with plan while investing and delivering operating efficiencies.
  • Provision for credit losses: strong overall asset quality and year-on-year improvement expected in a normalized environment.
  • Profitability: on track to deliver strong operating performance in 2026.
  • Capital: current share repurchase program underway; deductions from CET1 capital in line with raised payout ratio target of 60%.

data.title: A10 Networks reiterates 2026 outlook of 10%-12% revenue growth amid AI infrastructure build-outs
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: A10 Networks (ATEN) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “A10 continued to deliver on our strategic plan centered around the current AI-driven demand cycle while simultaneously focusing on disciplined execution,” said Board Chair, President & CEO Dhrupad Trivedi, adding that customers are focused on “accelerating traffic volume and complexity and emerging security threats in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.”

  • “We delivered 13.4% revenue growth in the first quarter,” said President Trivedi, and highlighted concentration from a major deployment: “This quarter, we were selected as a technology partner for a new application at one of the most significant AI infrastructure build-outs in our industry. As a result, the customer behind this build-out represents a high percent of total revenue this quarter.”

  • “AI is transforming the distinction between how large enterprises and service providers build their networks,” President Trivedi said, framing the strategy as “one architecture, one operating model, one security framework across both segments.”

  • “Q1 results were aligned with our business model goals and delivered revenue growth of 13.4% to $75 million,” said Chief Financial Officer Michelle Caron, adding that “product revenue was $44 million or 59% of total revenue.”

  • “Non-GAAP gross margin was 80.6%,” CFO Caron said, while noting investment levels: “Operating expenses were $41.5 million as we prioritized investments in AI-facing innovation, next-gen networking and security.”

Outlook

  • “We are reiterating our 2026 outlook with 2026 revenue growth within our guided range of 10% to 12%, adjusted EBITDA margins between 28% to 30% and EPS growth of 12% to 14%,” said Board Chair, President & CEO Dhrupad Trivedi.

  • On why guidance was unchanged despite Q1 growth, President Trivedi said, “we want to see the progression through the year,” and cited items the company is “navigating,” including “supply, lead times and cost challenges for some components” and that “our EMEA business has a little impact from some conflicts there.”

  • No prior-quarter transcript was provided beyond a “not available” placeholder, so changes versus the prior quarter’s guidance language could not be verified from the supplied materials.

Financial Results

  • “Operating margin was 25.2%, resulting in net income of $17.7 million or $0.25 per basic and $0.24 per diluted share,” CFO Michelle Caron said, adding that “Q1 diluted weighted share count was 72.9 million shares.”

  • On mix and demand drivers, CFO Caron said, “security-led revenue was a strong driver of our product revenue growth,” and “enterprise customers represented 56% of Q1 revenues,” while “service provider revenue was 44% of total revenue in the first quarter.”

  • On geography and demand frictions, CFO Caron said, “our Americas region represented 67% of global revenue,” while “in EMEA, we saw headwinds related to regional conflicts” and “in APJ, spending remains conservative as customers navigate an uncertain capital environment.”

  • On cash flow timing and inventory, CFO Caron said, “operating cash flow and therefore, free cash flow in the quarter was temporarily impacted by the timing of receivables as well as inventory investments,” and added, “we expect both to normalize over the course of the year.”

  • On capital returns and liquidity, CFO Caron said, “cash and marketable securities were $369.7 million,” “deferred revenue was $147.2 million,” and “the Board has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.06 per share.”

Q&A

  • Gray Powell, BTIG, LLC, Research Division: “where do you think we’re at in the investment cycle around AI?” and whether A10 would “prioritiz[e] faster revenue growth over the historical 28% to 30% EBITDA margin framework” | Board Chair, President & CEO Trivedi: “we are in the midst of that build-out phase,” while “the second part of that cycle… is very early stage,” and “if we see opportunities for significantly faster growth while still delivering EPS expansion, we continue to look at those.”

  • Gray Powell, BTIG, LLC, Research Division: asked for detail on the “large customer win” and enterprise vs. service provider growth | President Trivedi: “it’s a case of an existing large customer expanding their deployment,” and “it’s really around an enterprise application. So it’s not the DDoS-type product.”

  • Hamed Khorsand, BWS Financial Inc.: asked if accounts receivable build was tied to “this one large project” and whether payment was received | CFO Caron: “this is a calendar event and not a credit event,” with “no meaningful uptick in our aged receivables” and “no concessions on standard payment terms.”

  • Hamed Khorsand, BWS Financial Inc.: “why the hesitation to keep guidance unchanged” | President Trivedi: “we want to see the progression through the year,” and “if we see that momentum continuing in Q2 and beyond, obviously, we will revisit that.”

  • Michael Romanelli, Mizuho Securities USA LLC, Research Division: asked about “expanded commitments” beyond the large project and regional demand | President Trivedi: “many of our existing customers… are all beginning to allocate more spending and priority to AI,” and on geographies said Japan has “caution,” EMEA has a portion “quite challenged,” and in the Americas, AI-leaning customers are “spending more” while telco is “very stable right now.”

  • Anja Soderstrom, Sidoti & Company, LLC: asked why “services have been lagging” versus product growth | President Trivedi: “product growing faster will show up in service improving in 4 quarters later, roughly,” while also noting, “our renewal rate is fine, very stable.”

  • Anja Soderstrom, Sidoti & Company, LLC: asked about competitive dynamics and share gains | President Trivedi: “we really have not seen any significant changes since the last quarter or 2,” and “we feel… we are in a good competitive position.”

  • Benjamin Taxdahl, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC, Research Division: asked if 10% to 12% implies a quarterly “step function” and whether growth is concentrated | President Trivedi: “it’s broad market share,” and said reiteration was due to timing: “we had our Investor Day subsequent to the earnings call in Q1.”

  • W. Chiu, Raymond James & Associates, Inc., Research Division: asked about “supply chain and kind of memory shortages,” pricing actions, and whether large orders were pulled in | President Trivedi: “the memory is the biggest,” involving “price increase… lead time and allocation,” and said A10 tries to “split that with customers”; on pull-ins, he added, “I don’t think that’s the issue… it’s more our customers are looking at building out things fast, and we are trying to keep up with them.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly positive, with supportive framing such as “Congratulations on the very strong set of results,” while also pressing on durability of AI spending, customer concentration, and the decision to keep guidance unchanged.

  • Management’s tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks, emphasizing positioning in AI and security, and remained largely steady in Q&A, repeatedly pointing to staged AI demand (“build-out phase” versus “very early stage”) and execution constraints such as “lead times and cost challenges.”

  • A prior-quarter transcript was not available in the prompt, so tone shifts versus the prior quarter could not be directly compared.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • The previous quarter transcript was not provided (listed as “not available”), so quarter-over-quarter changes in guidance language, analyst focus, and management confidence could not be verified from the supplied transcripts.

Risks and Concerns

  • “We are seeing delivery and cost challenges related to pricing of certain components,” CFO Michelle Caron said, adding, “we entered this environment with strong supplier relationships, and we will keep evaluating the evolving market and adapt as needed.”

  • On regional demand risk, CFO Caron said, “in EMEA, we saw headwinds related to regional conflicts,” while “in APJ, spending remains conservative,” and President Trivedi said Japan’s spending profile is “getting pushed out more to the right.”

  • On customer concentration tied to AI build-outs, President Trivedi said the AI infrastructure customer “represents a high percent of total revenue this quarter,” and described the operational intensity as requiring “prioritized allocation of product, inventory and engineering resources.”

Final Takeaway

A10 positioned Q1 around AI infrastructure build-outs and security demand, with President Trivedi emphasizing that a major AI deployment made one customer “a high percent of total revenue this quarter,” while CFO Caron detailed $75 million in revenue, 80.6% non-GAAP gross margin, and $0.24 non-GAAP diluted EPS. Management reiterated its 2026 framework—10% to 12% revenue growth, 28% to 30% adjusted EBITDA margins, and 12% to 14% EPS growth—while flagging component cost and lead-time constraints, regional softness in EMEA/APJ, and timing impacts to cash flow that it expects to normalize over the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Norsk Hydro Non-GAAP EPS of NOK 2.07 beats by NOK 0.53, revenue of NOK 50.39B beats by NOK 100M
data.description:
• Norsk Hydro press release (NHYDY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of NOK 2.07 beats by NOK 0.53.
  • Revenue of NOK 50.39B (-11.7% Y/Y) beats by NOK 100M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA NOK 8.7 billion.
  • Operating capital build from higher prices and sales giving negative free cash flow of NOK 4 billion.
  • The twelve month adjusted RoaCE ended at 10.1 percent.
  • Adjusted net debt increased from NOK 18.2 billion to NOK 21.6 billion.

data.title: Banco Santander Q1 results lifted by disposal gain
data.description:
• Banco Santander (Brasil) S.A. (BSBR): Net profit rose 12% Y/Y to €3.56B.
  • Attributable profit reached €5.5B, up 60%, including a €1.9B net capital gain following the completion of the sale of Santander Bank Polska in January. 
  • Spain’s largest lender reported a 4% surge to total revenue to €15.14B, supported by net interest income of €11.02B, up 4%, and net fee income of €3.36B, which increased 6% on the back of stronger customer activity and volume growth across all global businesses.
  • The CET1 capital ratio improved to 14.4%, rising 1.5 percentage points over the past 12 months, supported by solid organic capital generation and the positive impact from the disposal of the Poland business.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing exits Arm Holdings with $231M share sale
data.description:
• Taiwan's leading chipmaker TSMC (TSM) sold its investment in Arm Holdings (ARM), fully exiting its holdings in the UK-based chip designer.
  • Its subsidiary TSMC Partners sold 1.11M shares of Arm Holdings (ARM) between April 28 and 29 at $207.65 apiece, generating roughly $231M in proceeds, Reuters reported, citing regulatory filings.
  • The transaction resulted in a $174M impact on earnings, and the company said it was a part of broader equity investment disposal strategy, aligning with its ongoing portfolio optimization efforts.

data.title: UBS beats top-line and bottom-line estimates;remains on track to deliver on financial objectives for 2026
data.description:
• UBS press release (UBS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.94 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $14.24B (+13.4% Y/Y) beats by $1.03B.
  • Return on CET1 capital was 16.8%, and 17.0% on an underlying basis.
  • Underlying PBT was USD 3,990m.
  • Outlook: "We expect second quarter net interest income in both Global Wealth Management and Personal & Corporate Banking to be broadly flat sequentially.

    The current backdrop reinforces the benefits of our balance sheet for all seasons, and we are confident in delivering on our 2026 financial targets while continuing to invest in sustainable growth and long-term value creation."
